threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you ask for directions to the nearest tube and get a blank stare—everyone here knows it’s bus or bike all the way.
Heard a guy on the 3 at 2 AM claiming he’s a ‘night bus philosopher’—kept quoting obscure poets while everyone else was half-asleep. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler in a stroller munching on a döner while waiting for the bus, I realized this is home. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see a spontaneous party break out at 3 AM in an abandoned train station, complete with DJs and dance circles. It’s wild.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their disdain for tourists stopping dead in the crosswalk just to snap a pic of a taco truck.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped mid-traffic because a pigeon decided it needed a nap on the tracks. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just about hip bars, but it's the local bakeries at dawn where the real magic happens—fresh pa de pessic that’ll knock your socks off.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they mispronounce 'Gràcia' – if they say 'Gra-sia,' they’re still figuring it out.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still hesitate to order a flat white because you think it’s just coffee, not a ten-minute debate on oat vs almond milk.
Caught a guy singing opera on the 69 last night. Whole bus joined in, even the driver flashed his lights in rhythm. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw an old gentleman feeding stray cats outside the Arabic bakery at dawn, it hit me—this place has its own heartbeat.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a bar that serves homemade limoncello while hosting a punk show on a Tuesday. It's chaos, but it's ours.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by the way they casually ignore the Civic Center BART chaos, while a newcomer is panicking over the smoke and sirens.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the Muni crowd with a saxophone, just as the bus lurched to a stop. Whole bus erupted in applause, then he vanished into the fog.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists miss that Gràcia’s plazas are alive after 9 PM, filled with locals gathering for cheap wines and deep conversations about everything.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them trying to pay for everything in cash, clueless about the custom of using contactless cards on the metro. Classic rookie move.
